core_principle.statement: Execution is a controlled engineering process, not a construction activity.
core_principle.interpretation: Every architectural system must pass through structured engineering execution phases to ensure buildability and reliability.

execution_philosophy.logic_flow: Design → Engineering Validation → System Definition → Manufacturing → Installation → Verification

execution_pipeline.description: Receive architectural intent and spatial requirements
Analyze feasibility, loads, geometry, and constraints
execution_pipeline.rule: All designs must pass engineering checks before systemization

execution_pipeline.description: Convert validated design into modular ceiling system architecture
execution_pipeline.rule: Everything must become a structured system
Manufacturing follows system logic, not ad-hoc production
Installation must follow system documentation exactly
No project is complete without system verification

execution_control_system.risk_control: Pre-execution validation of all system components
execution_control_system.documentation: Full traceability from design to installation

engineering_constraints.geometry_control: Must follow AECS geometry and grid system
engineering_constraints.modularity: All components must be system-based modules
engineering_constraints.consistency: No deviation from approved engineering model

site_behavior_rules.installation_discipline: Execution teams follow engineering documentation only
site_behavior_rules.system_first: All decisions must reference system documentation

integration_with_brand_system.product_system_link: Execution is final phase of Product Architecture System
integration_with_brand_system.design_system_link: Execution validates Design → Engineering → System flow

user_perception: This company controls execution like an engineering system

strategic_goal.objective: Transform construction into controlled engineering execution
strategic_goal.outcome: AvizSazeh is perceived as an execution-grade architectural engineering system provider
